req.title: Standing order (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall set up a standing order with start date in the future; past start dates shall be rejected. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.
preconditions: authenticated

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-045 mandates that standing orders require a future start_date; today and past dates must yield 422. Three equivalence partitions exist: (1) start_date > today [valid], (2) start_date == today [invalid boundary], (3) start_date < today [invalid]. BVA targets today-1, today, and today+1 as the three critical boundary points. A positive EP test uses a clearly future date (today+30) to confirm 201 scheduling. A boundary BVA test uses today+1 (the minimum valid date, one step inside the valid partition) to confirm 201. A negative NEG test uses today (exact boundary, invalid side) to confirm 422. An additional negative BVA test uses today-1 (past date) to confirm 422. A DT test covers a combination of invalid amount with a valid future date to ensure validation is independent. State Transition Coverage is applied to verify the order moves from PENDING to SCHEDULED only when the date is valid.

tests.name: test_standing_order_future_date_succeeds
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth_token fixture is available
Banking API is reachable at BASE_URL
start_date is 30 days in the future (well inside valid partition)
actions: Build payload with start_date = today + 30 days, amount=100, currency=GBP, frequency=MONTHLY
expected: Response body status field equals SCHEDULED
trace: R-FIN-045: future start dates shall be accepted
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import requests

def test_standing_order_future_date_succeeds(auth_token, base_url):
    start_date = (date.today() + timedelta(days=30)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "start_date": start_date,
        "amount": 100,
        "currency": "GBP",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/standing-orders",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "order_id" in body, "Missing order_id in response"
    assert body.get("status") == "SCHEDULED", f"Unexpected status: {body.get('status')}"

tests.name: test_standing_order_today_date_rejected
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth_token fixture is available
Banking API is reachable at BASE_URL
start_date is set to today (invalid: not in the future)
actions: Build payload with start_date = today, amount=100, currency=GBP, frequency=MONTHLY
expected: Response body contains error code referencing invalid start_date
trace: R-FIN-045: past start dates (including today) shall be rejected with 422
NEG: start_date == today is explicitly cited as invalid in error pathways
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date
import requests

def test_standing_order_today_date_rejected(auth_token, base_url):
    start_date = date.today().isoformat()
    payload = {
        "start_date": start_date,
        "amount": 100,
        "currency": "GBP",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/standing-orders",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "start_date" in str(body).lower(), "Error body should reference start_date"

tests.name: test_standing_order_tomorrow_date_accepted_boundary
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth_token fixture is available
Banking API is reachable at BASE_URL
start_date is today+1 (minimum valid boundary value)
actions: Build payload with start_date = today + 1 day, amount=50, currency=GBP, frequency=WEEKLY
expected: Response body status field equals SCHEDULED
trace: R-FIN-045: boundary behaviour at exact min threshold must be tested
BVA: today+1 is the minimum allowable start_date
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import requests

def test_standing_order_tomorrow_date_accepted_boundary(auth_token, base_url):
    start_date = (date.today() + timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "start_date": start_date,
        "amount": 50,
        "currency": "GBP",
        "frequency": "WEEKLY"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/standing-orders",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 at min boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "order_id" in body, "Missing order_id at min boundary"
    assert body.get("status") == "SCHEDULED"

tests.name: test_standing_order_past_date_rejected_boundary
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth_token fixture is available
Banking API is reachable at BASE_URL
start_date is today-1 (one step below the invalid boundary)
actions: Build payload with start_date = today - 1 day, amount=100, currency=GBP, frequency=MONTHLY
expected: Response body references start_date validation error
trace: R-FIN-045: past start dates shall be rejected; boundary behaviour at exact thresholds must be tested
BVA: today-1 is the first invalid value below the boundary
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import requests

def test_standing_order_past_date_rejected_boundary(auth_token, base_url):
    start_date = (date.today() - timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "start_date": start_date,
        "amount": 100,
        "currency": "GBP",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/standing-orders",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422 for past date boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "start_date" in str(body).lower(), "Error body should reference start_date"

tests.name: test_standing_order_valid_date_invalid_amount_rejected
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth_token fixture is available
Banking API is reachable at BASE_URL
start_date is valid (future) but amount is zero (invalid)
actions: Build payload with start_date = today + 5 days, amount=0, currency=GBP, frequency=MONTHLY
expected: Response body references amount validation error, not start_date
trace: R-FIN-045: validation rules are independent; a valid date does not override other field failures
DT: decision table row where date=valid AND amount=invalid => 422 on amount
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import requests

def test_standing_order_valid_date_invalid_amount_rejected(auth_token, base_url):
    start_date = (date.today() + timedelta(days=5)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "start_date": start_date,
        "amount": 0,
        "currency": "GBP",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/standing-orders",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422 for zero amount, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "amount" in str(body).lower(), "Error body should reference amount field"

tests.name: test_standing_order_transitions_to_scheduled_state
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and auth_token fixture is available
Banking API is reachable at BASE_URL
No prior order exists with same idempotency key
start_date is 14 days in the future
actions: POST /standing-orders with valid future start_date and unique idempotency key
Assert state transition from implied PENDING creation to SCHEDULED
expected: POST returns HTTP 201 with status SCHEDULED
trace: R-FIN-045: order scheduled is the expected outcome for valid input
STC: verifies the order entity transitions correctly to SCHEDULED state after creation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid
from datetime import date, timedelta
import requests

def test_standing_order_transitions_to_scheduled_state(auth_token, base_url):
    start_date = (date.today() + timedelta(days=14)).isoformat()
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "start_date": start_date,
        "amount": 200,
        "currency": "GBP",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY"
    }
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}",
        "Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key
    }
    post_resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/standing-orders", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201, f"Creation failed: {post_resp.text}"
    order_id = post_resp.json()["order_id"]
    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/standing-orders/{order_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json().get("status") == "SCHEDULED"
